content: We can frame the basic Self-Refine iterative improvement framework as a foundational scaffold that can be augmented with multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, to create a multi-perspective self-refinement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while fixing its blind spot of single-perspective feedback bias. This hybrid system would leverage the tension between inconsistent solution perspectives to generate more rigorous self-feedback than the standard single-stream iterative refinement of vanilla Self-Refine.
